event_summary: Russia launched its largest two-day aerial attack of the war—over 1,500 drones and 56 missiles—while Ukraine's anti-corruption court ordered the arrest of Andriy Yermak, Zelensky's former chief of staff, on money-laundering charges with bail set at $3 million.
tldr: Russia's largest aerial offensive of the war—over 1,500 drones and 56 missiles—is the anchor frame across nearly all 24 buckets, but the Yermak money-laundering arrest appears in ten buckets and drives the CRIME_PUNISHMENT frame. Iraq is the most isolated bucket (0.400) with an article about US economic resilience to Iran war shocks—semantically disconnected from Ukraine war content, a clear pipeline artefact. Iran_opposition uniquely reports that Yermak consulted a fortune-teller for government appointments—a detail in no other bucket. Russia bucket is fourth-most isolated (0.510), filing entirely on the Belgorod and Bryansk governor replacements rather than the missile barrage—an inward-looking domestic narrative that avoids the international coverage angle entirely. No paradox was identified.

frames.sub_frame: drone barrage, Kyiv strikes, air defense

evidence.quote: Russia unleashes 1,500 attack drones on Ukraine, shattering prospects for peace
Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, killing 1 and injuring 31
Ukraine has escalated its campaign against Russian air defense systems, using expanding drone production and more advanced strike technologies
Andriy Yermak is suspected in an alleged 460-million-hryvnia scheme
Andriy Yermak once mingled with world leaders; now he’s languishing in a Kyiv cell over money-laundering charges.
Andriy Yermak, who is a suspect in a major corruption scandal, consulted a fortune-teller when deciding on top government appointments
After six years, governance under a state of danger has come to an end in Hungary.
President Vladimir Putin replaced the governors of the Bryansk and Belgorod regions on Wednesday, installing a military general and a former official in occupied Ukraine
Latvian Prime Minister Silina resigns over handling of Ukraine drone incidents
The EU is sanctioning 16 individuals and seven entities over alleged child abductions.
an oil tanker sailing under the name Grace Felix arrived in the Bay of Durres carrying 21,000 tonnes of gasoil
European External Action Service Deputy Secretary General Matti Maasikas defended the EU's sanctions policy on Tuesday

Ukraine’s Business Recovery Index turned negative for the first time since March 2023, dropping to -0.11 in April as order portfolios collapsed to their shortest in months and medium-term uncertainty climbed

isolation_top.note: Article is about US economic resilience to Iran war shocks—off-topic; corpus artefact from multi-story pipeline overlap
Vatican News covers only a Ukrainian war mother's grief narrative—personal-loss register semantically distant from military/political corpus
Shadow fleet tanker investigation (Grace Felix, Durres)—shipping logistics frame isolated from war-news corpus
Files entirely on Belgorod/Bryansk governor replacements—domestic political story that avoids international missile-barrage coverage
Combines NATO sanctions defense with Bornholm/NATO-testing essay—strategic analysis register
NATO tech accelerator launch at B9 summit and Stubb press conference—capacity-building angle distinct from kinetic-war corpus

exclusive_vocab_highlights.what_it_reveals: Gladkov's resignation and replacements in Belgorod and Bryansk—Russia bucket covers frontline governor reshuffles rather than the barrage, signaling inward domestic framing
Veronika Anikyevich fortune-teller detail in the Yermak corruption story—a colorful governance-failure angle unique to this bucket
Ukraine's Business Recovery Index turning negative—economic-data story entirely absent from every other bucket
Grace Felix shadow-fleet tanker investigation in Albania—sanctions evasion reporting unique to Balkans press
NATO DIANA tech accelerator launch at B9 summit—defense-technology capacity angle exclusive to Nordic coverage

silences.what_they_covered_instead: China is a key diplomatic actor whose position on a Ukraine settlement matters but is entirely absent from this corpus
France provides significant military support to Ukraine and is a major European actor on the war; its absence leaves European military-supply framing unrepresented
Iran's arms-supply relationship with Russia (drones) would be a natural angle but is absent from the corpus

single_outlet_findings.finding: Vatican News covers only a Ukrainian war mother's grief narrative (Lyubov, whose son Oleksandr died February 2024)—zero military analysis; the most humanized register in the corpus
Article is entirely about US economic resilience to Iran war shocks—a misrouted corpus entry with no Ukraine war content; most isolated entry (0.400)
Only outlet to report that Yermak consulted a fortune-teller named Veronika Anikyevich when deciding government appointments—detail absent from all other coverage
Only bucket to cover the NATO DIANA tech accelerator launched at the B9 summit—defense-technology capacity story that reframes the war as a driver of European innovation

bottom_line: Russia's largest aerial offensive of the war ran parallel to the arrest of Zelensky's most powerful former aide—two stories that frame Ukraine's predicament from opposite directions: military pressure from without and corruption pressure from within. China, France, and Iran_state are all absent from the corpus, leaving three actors with direct influence on any eventual peace settlement entirely unrepresented.
